task_completion.description: The agent opens the Add New Item dialog to create the manifest file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add New Item' dialog box is visible in Visual Studio, ideally with 'Application Manifest File' selected or being searched for.

task_completion.description: The app.manifest file is added to the SecurityLogAnalyzer project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Solution Explorer pane displays 'app.manifest' as a file under the SecurityLogAnalyzer project tree.

task_completion.description: The app.manifest file is opened in the Visual Studio editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A document tab named 'app.manifest' is active in the main Visual Studio text editor area.

task_completion.description: The requested execution level in the manifest is set to requireAdministrator.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The XML code visible in the editor shows the <requestedExecutionLevel> tag with the attribute level="requireAdministrator".

task_completion.description: The solution is successfully built after configuring the manifest.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Visual Studio Output pane or the bottom status bar explicitly displays a success message such as 'Build succeeded' or 'Build: 1 succeeded, 0 failed'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Visual Studio GUI to add the file and build the project, rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visual Studio menus, dialog boxes, and the Solution Explorer are visible in the screenshots, with no evidence of PowerShell or Command Prompt being used to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The manifest file was edited inside Visual Studio.
integrity.visual_evidence: The XML editing occurs within the Visual Studio IDE's text editor, not in an external text editor like Notepad.

integrity.description: The build success message is genuine and generated by Visual Studio.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Build succeeded' text appears in the standard, read-only Visual Studio Output window or status bar, not typed into a text file or mocked.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target project directory structure requirements and expected executable file paths match the explicit instructions provided in the prompt description.
